flashcards.front: Formula for Weighted Average Cost of Capital (WACC)
flashcards.back: WACC = (E/V x Re) + ((D/V x Rd) x (1 - T)). Where E=Equity, D=Debt, V=Total Value, Re=Cost of Equity, Rd=Cost of Debt, T=Tax Rate.

questions.question: A project requires an initial investment of 100,000. It generates cash flows of 50,000, 40,000, and 30,000 at the end of years 1, 2, and 3. If the discount rate is 10%, calculate the NPV (round to nearest whole number if applicable but input exact float for scoring compatibility up to 2 decimal places, e.g., xxx.xx).
